build_brief.decision_statement: Build a salesforce report for the manager persona to answer: Manager owner list report for renewals needing follow-up this week.

page_blueprint.purpose: End in an actionable queue or follow-up path.

critic_rationale.expectation: Choose the right surface first, then use handoffs only where they sharpen the action path.
findings_before: The excellence target expects a crma_dashboard handoff.
constraints: Keep the build on the chosen primary surface unless the planner is explicitly overridden again.
Preserve the named handoff only when it sharpens the action path.
strengths_after: Primary surface matches the builder default for this ask.
Secondary handoff surface matches the local excellence pattern.
Draft page model is aligned with a proven local surface pattern.

critic_rationale.expectation: Keep the page model compact and make the narrative order obvious to the audience.
constraints: Keep the page model within the planned budget and preserve the narrative order.
Do not expand the asset into extra pages or summary clutter without a new story review.
strengths_after: Draft page model is aligned with a proven local surface pattern.

critic_rationale.expectation: Every operating surface should end in a real queue, handoff, or follow-up path.
findings_before: The excellence target expects a crma_dashboard handoff.

revisions_applied.reason: Added the preferred secondary handoff surface from the excellence target.
Kept the report tied to a richer follow-up surface so the action path stays real.

constraints: Preserve the real queue, action layer, or follow-up handoff path.
Do not remove the explicit operating path from the final surface.
strengths_after: Secondary handoff surface matches the local excellence pattern.
Report format is queue-first and aligned to operating use.
Draft includes an explicit queue or action path for the operating persona.

critic_rationale.expectation: Use visual forms that match the surface: scan-fast story for CRMA executive views, queue-first tables for reports, lightweight rollups for native dashboards.

revisions_applied.reason: Added actual/variance/risk/action labeling to metric roles.

constraints: Keep the visual grammar aligned to the audience and surface type.
Do not reintroduce generic queue-like headline widgets or unlabeled metrics.
strengths_after: Draft has already been promoted through a critic-driven revision pass.

phases.objective: Author the report exactly as packaged.
actions: Expose columns: Owner, Account, Renewal Date, Risk Band, Product Family, Actual Ownership Alignment, Variance Driver: Forecast Hygiene.
Sort by: Action Queue: Handoff Quality, Owner.

phases.objective: Preserve the follow-up path and validate the queue.
actions: Validate filter vocabulary, row-level accountability, and screenshot fit before rollout.

acceptance_criteria: Primary surface remains salesforce_report and stays aligned to the builder decision.
Metric roles remain explicitly labeled as actual, variance/driver, risk, and action.
Handoff path is real and points to crma_dashboard.
Final surface stays within the planned page/view budget of 1.
Report remains queue-first and keeps the specified grouping, columns, and sort order.
Final asset passes live data validation and screenshot review before rollout.
next_steps: Author the report with the specified format, grouping, filters, columns, and sort order.
Keep the report queue-first; do not turn it into an executive story surface.
Preserve the crma_dashboard handoff for richer follow-up diagnostics.
Validate the filter vocabulary and row-level action path before rollout.
